role: Program Coordinator, Development Impact Evaluation (DIME) & UNWFP
company: World Bank Group & World Food Programme (UNWFP)
tasks: Coordinating data collection activities, community mobilization and training, monitoring data quality, and data management including conducting necessary back-checks
Strategically planned community/beneficiary mobilization efforts in various stages of project implementation initiatives

Monitored ground-level project implementation, promptly updating and collaborating with the project team on any alterations or threats to the project/ evaluation design and working on required changes for approval and implementation.
Liaising as focal point between various project stakeholders impact evaluation-related activities for decision-making
Played a central role in training, preparation and coordination of project implementation staff in various capacities
Coordinated communication between the overall project implementation team and field activity coordination teams for seamless operations

Designed and deployed mobile phone surveys to collect passenger feedback for the SmartMatatu Project, focusing on road safety in the minibus industry in Kenya in collaboration with the University of California, Berkley research team.
Collaborated on the design and development of innovative ideas, methods, and treatments using SmartMatatu vehicle tracker technology to collect safety data.
Managed project budgets and timelines across various facets, preparing necessary reports and updates.
Coordinated relationships with relevant stakeholders in various SACCOs (minibus management companies) and other local/county government stakeholders.
Configured, supervised, and monitored the installation and use of vehicle trackers on minibusses across Kenya.
Led and coordinated a team of enumerators responsible for data collection and road safety awareness campaigns.
Provided support to the research team with data analysis using tools such as Excel and R.

company: University of California, Berkeley (UCB) - Economics Research
tasks: Monitored and coordinated various project stages, including data collection, daily data analysis and exploration, and steps to ensure high-quality data production using tools such as Excel, R and Python
Established and maintained trust with key personnel at Jumia-Kenya for smooth data sharing and project activity execution
Analyzed data from SMEs onboarded onto the platform and created reports on project progress and results from customer and business engagements.

Installed and facilitated the use of various software/plugins to develop various customer chat interface environments, investigating customer reactions based on metrics such as agent gender and categories of customer issues
Collaborated closely with project Principal Investigators (PIs) in preparing research findings for donor reporting and planning project activities.
